1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Bm25 Index
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: BM25 trigger phrase re-index gate
5: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
6: import { normalizeContentForBM25 } from '../parsing/content-normalizer.js';
7: import {
8:   normalizeLexicalQueryTokens,
9:   sanitizeFTS5Query,
10:   sanitizeQueryTokens,
11:   simpleStem,
12:   tokenize,
13: } from './lexical-normalizer.js';
14: import type { NormalizedLexicalQueryTokens } from './lexical-normalizer.js';
15: import { clearRegisteredTimer, registerTimeout } from '../runtime/timer-registry.js';
16: 
17: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18: // 1. INTERFACES
19: 
20: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: interface BM25SearchResult {
22:   id: string;
23:   /**
24:    * BM25 term-frequency relevance score (unbounded, typically 0-25+).
25:    * Higher = better lexical match. Not directly comparable to vector similarity
26:    * scores; use min-max normalization or RRF when combining with other methods.
27:    */
28:   score: number;
29: }
30: 
31: interface BM25Stats {
32:   documentCount: number;
33:   termCount: number;
34:   avgDocLength: number;
35: }
36: 
37: interface BM25DocumentSource {
38:   title?: string | null;
39:   content_text?: string | null;
40:   trigger_phrases?: string | string[] | null;
41:   file_path?: string | null;
42: }
43: 
44: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45:    1B. CONSTANTS & FEATURE FLAG
46:    ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
47: 
48: const DEFAULT_K1 = 1.2;
49: const DEFAULT_B = 0.75;
50: const BM25_WARMUP_BATCH_SIZE = 250;
51: const BM25_ENABLED_VALUES = new Set(['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on', 'experimental', 'fallback']);
52: const BM25_DISABLED_VALUES = new Set(['0', 'false', 'no', 'off']);
53: const BM25_ENGINE_VALUES = ['auto', 'sqlite', 'packed-inmemory', 'legacy-inmemory'] as const;
54: type Bm25Engine = typeof BM25_ENGINE_VALUES[number];
55: let packedInmemoryWarningEmitted = false;
56: 
57: /**
58:  * Field weight multipliers for weighted BM25 scoring.
59:  * When indexing a document composed of multiple fields, multiply
60:  * each field's token scores by the appropriate weight before
61:  * accumulating the total document score.
62:  *
63:  * These weights are consumed by the FTS5 path in sqlite-fts.ts,
64:  * not the in-memory BM25 engine in this file. Exported for shared access.
65:  *
66:  * title:           10.0 — exact title matches are the strongest signal
67:  * trigger_phrases:  5.0 — curated keywords next most important
68:  * content_generic:  2.0 — generic textual content (file_path, tags, etc.)
69:  * body:             1.0 — baseline weight for full body / content_text
70:  */
71: const BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS = [10.0, 5.0, 2.0, 1.0] as const;
72: 
73: const BM25_FIELD_WEIGHTS: Record<string, number> = {
74:   title: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[0],
75:   trigger_phrases: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[1],
76:   content_generic: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[2],
77:   body: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[3],
78: };
79: 
80: /**
81:  * Check whether the in-memory BM25 index is enabled.
82:  *
83:  * @returns `true` when BM25 indexing/search is enabled for the current process.
84:  * @example
85:  * ```ts
86:  * if (isBm25Enabled()) {
87:  *   getIndex();
88:  * }
89:  * ```
90:  */
91: function isBm25Enabled(): boolean {
92:   const value = process.env.ENABLE_BM25?.trim().toLowerCase();
93:   if (!value) return true; // enabled by default
94:   if (BM25_DISABLED_VALUES.has(value)) return false;
95:   return BM25_ENABLED_VALUES.has(value);
96: }
97: 
98: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99: // 2. HELPERS
100: 
101: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
102: /**
103:  * Count token frequency occurrences for BM25 scoring.
104:  *
105:  * @param tokens - Normalized tokens produced by {@link tokenize}.
106:  * @returns A frequency map keyed by token text.
107:  * @example
108:  * ```ts
109:  * getTermFrequencies(['memory', 'memory', 'search']).get('memory');
110:  * // 2
111:  * ```
112:  */
113: function getTermFrequencies(tokens: string[]): Map<string, number> {
114:   const freq = new Map<string, number>();
115:   for (const token of tokens) {
116:     freq.set(token, (freq.get(token) || 0) + 1);
117:   }
118:   return freq;
119: }
120: 
121: function normalizeTriggerPhrasesForBM25(triggerPhrases: string | string[] | null | undefined): string {
122:   if (Array.isArray(triggerPhrases)) {
123:     return triggerPhrases
124:       .filter((phrase): phrase is string => typeof phrase === 'string' && phrase.trim().length > 0)
125:       .join(' ');
126:   }
127: 
128:   if (typeof triggerPhrases !== 'string') {
129:     return '';
130:   }
131: 
132:   const trimmed = triggerPhrases.trim();
133:   if (!trimmed) {
134:     return '';
135:   }
136: 
137:   try {
138:     const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed) as unknown;
139:     if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
140:       return parsed
141:         .filter((phrase): phrase is string => typeof phrase === 'string' && phrase.trim().length > 0)
142:         .join(' ');
143:     }
144:   } catch {
145:     // Fall back to raw string when the database value is not JSON.
146:   }
147: 
148:   return trimmed;
149: }
150: 
151: function buildBm25DocumentText(row: BM25DocumentSource): string {
152:   const textParts: string[] = [];
153: 
154:   if (typeof row.title === 'string' && row.title.trim()) {
155:     textParts.push(row.title.trim());
156:   }
157: 
158:   if (typeof row.content_text === 'string' && row.content_text.trim()) {
159:     textParts.push(normalizeContentForBM25(row.content_text));
160:   }
161: 
162:   const triggerPhrases = normalizeTriggerPhrasesForBM25(row.trigger_phrases);
163:   if (triggerPhrases) {
164:     textParts.push(triggerPhrases);
165:   }
166: 
167:   if (typeof row.file_path === 'string' && row.file_path.trim()) {
168:     textParts.push(row.file_path.trim());
169:   }
170: 
171:   return textParts.join(' ').trim();
172: }
173: 
174: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
175: // 3. BM25 INDEX CLASS
176: 
177: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
178: class BM25Index {
179:   private k1: number;
180:   private b: number;
181:   private documents: Map<string, { tokens: string[]; termFreq: Map<string, number> }>;
182:   private documentFreq: Map<string, number>;
183:   private totalDocLength: number;
184:   private warmupHandle: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null;
185:   private warmupGeneration: number;
186: 
187:   constructor(k1: number = DEFAULT_K1, b: number = DEFAULT_B) {
188:     this.k1 = k1;
189:     this.b = b;
190:     this.documents = new Map();
191:     this.documentFreq = new Map();
192:     this.totalDocLength = 0;
193:     this.warmupHandle = null;
194:     this.warmupGeneration = 0;
195:   }
196: 
197:   addDocument(id: string, text: string): void {
198:     const tokens = tokenize(text);
199:     const termFreq = getTermFrequencies(tokens);
200: 
201:     // Remove old document if exists
202:     if (this.documents.has(id)) {
203:       this.removeDocument(id);
204:     }
205: 
206:     this.documents.set(id, { tokens, termFreq });
207:     this.totalDocLength += tokens.length;
208: 
209:     // Update document frequency
210:     for (const term of termFreq.keys()) {
211:       this.documentFreq.set(term, (this.documentFreq.get(term) || 0) + 1);
212:     }
213:   }
214: 
215:   removeDocument(id: string): boolean {
216:     const doc = this.documents.get(id);
217:     if (!doc) return false;
218: 
219:     this.totalDocLength -= doc.tokens.length;
220: 
221:     for (const term of doc.termFreq.keys()) {
222:       const count = this.documentFreq.get(term) || 0;
223:       if (count <= 1) {
224:         this.documentFreq.delete(term);
225:       } else {
226:         this.documentFreq.set(term, count - 1);
227:       }
228:     }
229: 
230:     this.documents.delete(id);
231:     return true;
232:   }
233: 
234:   calculateIdf(term: string): number {
235:     const n = this.documents.size;
236:     const df = this.documentFreq.get(term) || 0;
237:     if (df === 0 || n === 0) return 0;
238:     return Math.log((n - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1);
239:   }
240: 
241:   calculateScore(queryTokens: string[], docId: string): number {
242:     const doc = this.documents.get(docId);
243:     if (!doc) return 0;
244: 
245:     const avgDl = this.documents.size > 0 ? this.totalDocLength / this.documents.size : 0;
246:     const dl = doc.tokens.length;
247: 
248:     let score = 0;
249:     for (const term of queryTokens) {
250:       const tf = doc.termFreq.get(term) || 0;
251:       if (tf === 0) continue;
252: 
253:       const idf = this.calculateIdf(term);
254:       const numerator = tf * (this.k1 + 1);
255:       const denominator = tf + this.k1 * (1 - this.b + this.b * (dl / Math.max(avgDl, 1)));
256:       score += idf * (numerator / denominator);
257:     }
258: 
259:     return score;
260:   }
261: 
262:   search(query: string, limit: number = 10): BM25SearchResult[] {
263:     const queryTokens = normalizeLexicalQueryTokens(query).bm25;
264:     if (queryTokens.length === 0) return [];
265: 
266:     const results: BM25SearchResult[] = [];
267: 
268:     for (const [id] of this.documents) {
269:       const score = this.calculateScore(queryTokens, id);
270:       if (score > 0) {
271:         results.push({ id, score });
272:       }
273:     }
274: 
275:     return results
276:       .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
277:       .slice(0, limit);
278:   }
279: 
280:   getStats(): BM25Stats {
281:     return {
282:       documentCount: this.documents.size,
283:       termCount: this.documentFreq.size,
284:       avgDocLength: this.documents.size > 0 ? this.totalDocLength / this.documents.size : 0,
285:     };
286:   }
287: 
288:   clear(): void {
289:     this.cancelWarmup();
290:     this.documents.clear();
291:     this.documentFreq.clear();
292:     this.totalDocLength = 0;
293:   }
294: 
295:   addDocuments(docs: Array<{ id: string; text: string }>): void {
296:     for (const doc of docs) {
297:       this.addDocument(doc.id, doc.text);
298:     }
299:   }
300: 
301:   /**
302:    * Incrementally synchronize changed rows from the database into the in-memory index.
303:    */
304:   syncChangedRows(database: Database.Database, rowIds: Array<number | string>): number {
305:     const normalizedIds = Array.from(
306:       new Set(
307:         rowIds
308:           .map((rowId) => Number(rowId))
309:           .filter((rowId) => Number.isInteger(rowId) && rowId > 0)
310:       )
311:     );
312: 
313:     if (normalizedIds.length === 0) {
314:       return 0;
315:     }
316: 
317:     try {
318:       const placeholders = normalizedIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
319:       const rows = (database.prepare(
320:         `SELECT id, title, content_text, trigger_phrases, file_path
321:          FROM memory_index
322:          WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`
323:       ) as Database.Statement).all(...normalizedIds) as Array<{
324:         id: number;
325:         title: string | null;
326:         content_text: string | null;
327:         trigger_phrases: string | null;
328:         file_path: string | null;
329:       }>;
330: 
331:       const activeRowMap = new Map<number, typeof rows[number]>();
332:       for (const row of rows) {
333:         activeRowMap.set(row.id, row);
334:       }
335: 
336:       for (const rowId of normalizedIds) {
337:         const row = activeRowMap.get(rowId);
338:         if (!row) {
339:           this.removeDocument(String(rowId));
340:           continue;
341:         }
342: 
343:         const text = buildBm25DocumentText(row);
344:         if (text.trim()) {
345:           this.addDocument(String(row.id), text);
346:         } else {
347:           this.removeDocument(String(row.id));
348:         }
349:       }
350: 
351:       return normalizedIds.length;
352:     } catch (error: unknown) {
353:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
354:       console.warn(`[bm25-index] Failed to sync BM25 rows: ${msg}`);
355:       return 0;
356:     }
357:   }
358: 
359:   /**
360:    * Defer full startup warmup into batched row-ID syncs so process
361:    * initialization is not blocked by a monolithic in-memory rebuild.
362:    */
363:   rebuildFromDatabase(database: Database.Database): number {
364:     this.clear();
365: 
366:     try {
367:       const rows = (database.prepare(
368:         `SELECT id
369:          FROM memory_index
370:          ORDER BY id`
371:       ) as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{ id: number }>;
372: 
373:       const pendingIds = rows.map((row) => row.id);
374:       if (pendingIds.length === 0) {
375:         return 0;
376:       }
377: 
378:       const warmupGeneration = ++this.warmupGeneration;
379:       const processBatch = () => {
380:         if (warmupGeneration !== this.warmupGeneration) {
381:           return;
382:         }
383: 
384:         const batchIds = pendingIds.splice(0, BM25_WARMUP_BATCH_SIZE);
385:         if (batchIds.length === 0) {
386:           this.warmupHandle = null;
387:           return;
388:         }
389: 
390:         this.syncChangedRows(database, batchIds);
391: 
392:         if (pendingIds.length > 0) {
393:           this.warmupHandle = registerTimeout(processBatch, 0, { unref: true });
394:         } else {
395:           this.warmupHandle = null;
396:         }
397:       };
398: 
399:       this.warmupHandle = registerTimeout(processBatch, 0, { unref: true });
400:       return pendingIds.length;
401:     } catch (error: unknown) {
402:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
403:       console.warn(`[bm25-index] Failed to schedule BM25 warmup: ${msg}`);
404:       return 0;
405:     }
406:   }
407: 
408:   cancelWarmup(): void {
409:     this.warmupGeneration += 1;
410:     if (this.warmupHandle) {
411:       clearRegisteredTimer(this.warmupHandle);
412:       this.warmupHandle = null;
413:     }
414:   }
415: }
416: 
417: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
418: // 4. SINGLETON
419: 
420: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
421: let indexInstance: BM25Index | null = null;
422: 
423: /**
424:  * Retrieve the shared in-memory BM25 index singleton.
425:  *
426:  * @returns The process-wide {@link BM25Index} instance.
427:  * @example
428:  * ```ts
429:  * const index = getIndex();
430:  * index.addDocument('123', 'memory search');
431:  * ```
432:  */
433: function getIndex(): BM25Index {
434:   if (!indexInstance) {
435:     indexInstance = new BM25Index();
436:   }
437:   return indexInstance;
438: }
439: 
440: /**
441:  * Reset the shared BM25 index singleton.
442:  *
443:  * @returns Nothing.
444:  * @example
445:  * ```ts
446:  * resetIndex();
447:  * ```
448:  */
449: function resetIndex(): void {
450:   indexInstance?.cancelWarmup();
451:   indexInstance = null;
452: }
453: 
454: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
455: // 5. LEXICAL ENGINE ROUTING
456: 
457: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
458: function resolveBm25Engine(): Bm25Engine {
459:   const value = process.env.SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE?.trim().toLowerCase();
460:   if (!value) {
461:     return 'auto';
462:   }
463: 
464:   if ((BM25_ENGINE_VALUES as readonly string[]).includes(value)) {
465:     return value as Bm25Engine;
466:   }
467: 
468:   console.warn(`[bm25-index] Unsupported SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=${value}; falling back to auto`);
469:   return 'auto';
470: }
471: 
472: function isMemoryFtsAvailable(database: Database.Database | null | undefined): boolean {
473:   if (!database) {
474:     return false;
475:   }
476: 
477:   try {
478:     const result = (database.prepare(
479:       `SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'`
480:     ) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
481:     return !!result;
482:   } catch {
483:     return false;
484:   }
485: }
486: 
487: function emitPackedInmemoryWarning(): void {
488:   if (packedInmemoryWarningEmitted) {
489:     return;
490:   }
491:   packedInmemoryWarningEmitted = true;
492:   console.warn('[bm25-index] SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=packed-inmemory is reserved and not yet implemented; using legacy-inmemory');
493: }
494: 
495: function shouldWarmInMemoryBm25(database: Database.Database | null | undefined): boolean {
496:   if (!isBm25Enabled()) {
497:     return false;
498:   }
499: 
500:   const engine = resolveBm25Engine();
501:   if (engine === 'sqlite') {
502:     if (!isMemoryFtsAvailable(database)) {
503:       throw new Error('SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=sqlite requires the memory_fts table; JS BM25 warmup is disabled in sqlite mode');
504:     }
505:     return false;
506:   }
507: 
508:   if (engine === 'auto') {
509:     return !isMemoryFtsAvailable(database);
510:   }
511: 
512:   if (engine === 'packed-inmemory') {
513:     emitPackedInmemoryWarning();
514:     return true;
515:   }
516: 
517:   return true;
518: }
519: 
520: function shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine(database: Database.Database | null | undefined): boolean {
521:   const engine = resolveBm25Engine();
522:   if (engine === 'sqlite') {
523:     if (!isMemoryFtsAvailable(database)) {
524:       throw new Error('SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=sqlite requires the memory_fts table for lexical BM25 search');
525:     }
526:     return true;
527:   }
528: 
529:   if (engine === 'auto') {
530:     return isMemoryFtsAvailable(database);
531:   }
532: 
533:   if (engine === 'packed-inmemory') {
534:     emitPackedInmemoryWarning();
535:   }
536: 
537:   return false;
538: }
539: 
540: function getBm25EngineStatus(database: Database.Database | null | undefined = null): {
541:   lexical_engine: Bm25Engine;
542:   fts5_available: boolean;
543:   warms_in_memory_bm25: boolean;
544:   bm25_enabled: boolean;
545: } {
546:   const lexical_engine = resolveBm25Engine();
547:   const fts5_available = isMemoryFtsAvailable(database);
548:   let warms_in_memory_bm25 = false;
549: 
550:   try {
551:     warms_in_memory_bm25 = shouldWarmInMemoryBm25(database);
552:   } catch {
553:     warms_in_memory_bm25 = false;
554:   }
555: 
556:   return {
557:     lexical_engine,
558:     fts5_available,
559:     warms_in_memory_bm25,
560:     bm25_enabled: isBm25Enabled(),
561:   };
562: }
563: 
564: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
565: // 6. EXPORTS
566: 
567: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
568: export {
569:   BM25Index,
570:   getIndex,
571:   resetIndex,
572:   tokenize,
573:   simpleStem,
574:   getTermFrequencies,
575:   isBm25Enabled,
576:   resolveBm25Engine,
577:   isMemoryFtsAvailable,
578:   shouldWarmInMemoryBm25,
579:   shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine,
580:   getBm25EngineStatus,
581:   sanitizeQueryTokens,
582:   sanitizeFTS5Query,
583:   normalizeLexicalQueryTokens,
584:   buildBm25DocumentText,
585:   DEFAULT_K1,
586:   DEFAULT_B,
587:   BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS,
588:   BM25_FIELD_WEIGHTS,
589: };
590: 
591: export type {
592:   BM25SearchResult,
593:   BM25Stats,
594:   BM25DocumentSource,
595:   Bm25Engine,
596:   NormalizedLexicalQueryTokens,
597: };

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Query Plan
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Telemetry-only contract for explaining how query intelligence surfaces read a
5: // query. Builders in this module must not make routing decisions.
6: 
7: type QueryPlanIntent =
8:   | 'add_feature'
9:   | 'fix_bug'
10:   | 'refactor'
11:   | 'security_audit'
12:   | 'understand'
13:   | 'find_spec'
14:   | 'find_decision'
15:   | 'unknown';
16: 
17: type QueryPlanComplexity = 'simple' | 'moderate' | 'complex' | 'unknown';
18: 
19: type QueryPlanAuthorityNeed = 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'unknown';
20: 
21: type QueryPlanFallbackMode =
22:   | 'none'
23:   | 'safe_complexity'
24:   | 'full_pipeline'
25:   | 'classifier_default'
26:   | 'telemetry_only'
27:   | 'fts5_bm25_grep_broadening';
28: 
29: interface QueryPlanSkippedChannel {
30:   channel: string;
31:   reason: string;
32: }
33: 
34: interface QueryPlanFallbackPolicy {
35:   mode: QueryPlanFallbackMode;
36:   reason: string;
37:   tiers?: string[];
38:   deadlineMs?: number;
39: }
40: 
41: interface QueryPlan {
42:   intent: QueryPlanIntent;
43:   complexity: QueryPlanComplexity;
44:   artifactClass: string;
45:   authorityNeed: QueryPlanAuthorityNeed;
46:   selectedChannels: string[];
47:   skippedChannels: QueryPlanSkippedChannel[];
48:   routingReasons: string[];
49:   fallbackPolicy: QueryPlanFallbackPolicy;
50: }
51: 
52: interface BuildComplexityQueryPlanInput {
53:   complexity: QueryPlanComplexity;
54:   confidence: string;
55:   termCount?: number;
56:   hasTriggerMatch?: boolean;
57: }
58: 
59: interface BuildIntentQueryPlanInput {
60:   intent: string;
61:   confidence: number;
62:   keywords?: string[];
63:   query?: string;
64: }
65: 
66: interface BuildRoutingQueryPlanInput {
67:   query?: string;
68:   complexity: QueryPlanComplexity;
69:   artifactClass: string;
70:   selectedChannels: readonly string[];
71:   allChannels: readonly string[];
72:   intent?: string;
73:   authorityNeed?: QueryPlanAuthorityNeed;
74:   routingReasons?: readonly string[];
75:   fallbackPolicy?: QueryPlanFallbackPolicy;
76: }
77: 
78: const DEFAULT_FALLBACK_POLICY: QueryPlanFallbackPolicy = {
79:   mode: 'none',
80:   reason: 'No fallback applied',
81: };
82: 
83: const HIGH_AUTHORITY_ARTIFACTS = new Set([
84:   'spec',
85:   'plan',
86:   'tasks',
87:   'checklist',
88:   'decision-record',
89:   'implementation-summary',
90:   'research',
91: ]);
92: 
93: function createEmptyQueryPlan(overrides: Partial<QueryPlan> = {}): QueryPlan {
94:   const {
95:     selectedChannels,
96:     skippedChannels,
97:     routingReasons,
98:     fallbackPolicy,
99:     ...scalarOverrides
100:   } = overrides;
101: 
102:   return {
103:     intent: 'unknown',
104:     complexity: 'unknown',
105:     artifactClass: 'unknown',
106:     authorityNeed: 'unknown',
107:     ...scalarOverrides,
108:     selectedChannels: [...(selectedChannels ?? [])],
109:     skippedChannels: [...(skippedChannels ?? [])],
110:     routingReasons: [...(routingReasons ?? [])],
111:     fallbackPolicy: fallbackPolicy ?? DEFAULT_FALLBACK_POLICY,
112:   };
113: }
114: 
115: function normalizeIntent(intent: string | undefined): QueryPlanIntent {
116:   switch (intent) {
117:     case 'add_feature':
118:     case 'fix_bug':
119:     case 'refactor':
120:     case 'security_audit':
121:     case 'understand':
122:     case 'find_spec':
123:     case 'find_decision':
124:       return intent;
125:     default:
126:       return 'unknown';
127:   }
128: }
129: 
130: function inferAuthorityNeed(input: {
131:   intent?: string;
132:   artifactClass?: string;
133:   query?: string;
134: }): QueryPlanAuthorityNeed {
135:   const intent = normalizeIntent(input.intent);
136:   if (intent === 'security_audit' || intent === 'find_spec' || intent === 'find_decision') {
137:     return 'high';
138:   }
139: 
140:   const artifactClass = input.artifactClass ?? 'unknown';
141:   if (HIGH_AUTHORITY_ARTIFACTS.has(artifactClass)) {
142:     return 'high';
143:   }
144: 
145:   const query = input.query?.toLowerCase() ?? '';
146:   if (/\b(audit|security|decision|rationale|requirement|scope|checklist|citation|source)\b/.test(query)) {
147:     return 'high';
148:   }
149:   if (/\b(explain|understand|overview|how|why)\b/.test(query)) {
150:     return 'medium';
151:   }
152: 
153:   return 'low';
154: }
155: 
156: function mergeQueryPlans(...plans: Array<QueryPlan | undefined>): QueryPlan {
157:   const merged = createEmptyQueryPlan();
158: 
159:   for (const plan of plans) {
160:     if (!plan) continue;
161: 
162:     if (merged.intent === 'unknown' && plan.intent !== 'unknown') {
163:       merged.intent = plan.intent;
164:     }
165:     if (merged.complexity === 'unknown' && plan.complexity !== 'unknown') {
166:       merged.complexity = plan.complexity;
167:     }
168:     if (merged.artifactClass === 'unknown' && plan.artifactClass !== 'unknown') {
169:       merged.artifactClass = plan.artifactClass;
170:     }
171:     if (merged.authorityNeed === 'unknown' && plan.authorityNeed !== 'unknown') {
172:       merged.authorityNeed = plan.authorityNeed;
173:     }
174:     if (merged.fallbackPolicy.mode === 'none' && plan.fallbackPolicy.mode !== 'none') {
175:       merged.fallbackPolicy = plan.fallbackPolicy;
176:     }
177: 
178:     merged.selectedChannels = uniqueStrings([
179:       ...merged.selectedChannels,
180:       ...plan.selectedChannels,
181:     ]);
182:     merged.skippedChannels = mergeSkippedChannels(merged.skippedChannels, plan.skippedChannels);
183:     merged.routingReasons = uniqueStrings([
184:       ...merged.routingReasons,
185:       ...plan.routingReasons,
186:     ]);
187:   }
188: 
189:   return merged;
190: }
191: 
192: function buildComplexityQueryPlan(input: BuildComplexityQueryPlanInput): QueryPlan {
193:   const fallbackPolicy: QueryPlanFallbackPolicy = input.confidence === 'fallback'
194:     ? {
195:       mode: 'safe_complexity',
196:       reason: 'Complexity classifier fell back to safe complex tier',
197:     }
198:     : DEFAULT_FALLBACK_POLICY;
199: 
200:   const reasons = [
201:     `complexity:${input.complexity}`,
202:     `confidence:${input.confidence}`,
203:   ];
204:   if (typeof input.termCount === 'number') {
205:     reasons.push(`terms:${input.termCount}`);
206:   }
207:   if (input.hasTriggerMatch) {
208:     reasons.push('trigger-match');
209:   }
210: 
211:   return createEmptyQueryPlan({
212:     complexity: input.complexity,
213:     routingReasons: reasons,
214:     fallbackPolicy,
215:   });
216: }
217: 
218: function buildIntentQueryPlan(input: BuildIntentQueryPlanInput): QueryPlan {
219:   const intent = normalizeIntent(input.intent);
220:   const keywordReasons = (input.keywords ?? []).slice(0, 5).map((keyword) => `keyword:${keyword}`);
221:   return createEmptyQueryPlan({
222:     intent,
223:     authorityNeed: inferAuthorityNeed({ intent, query: input.query }),
224:     routingReasons: [
225:       `intent:${intent}`,
226:       `intent-confidence:${roundMetric(input.confidence)}`,
227:       ...keywordReasons,
228:     ],
229:   });
230: }
231: 
232: function buildRoutingQueryPlan(input: BuildRoutingQueryPlanInput): QueryPlan {
233:   const selected = uniqueStrings([...input.selectedChannels]);
234:   const allChannels = uniqueStrings([...input.allChannels]);
235:   const skippedChannels = allChannels
236:     .filter((channel) => !selected.includes(channel))
237:     .map((channel) => ({
238:       channel,
239:       reason: `Skipped by ${input.complexity} complexity route`,
240:     }));
241:   const intent = normalizeIntent(input.intent);
242:   const authorityNeed = input.authorityNeed ?? inferAuthorityNeed({
243:     intent,
244:     artifactClass: input.artifactClass,
245:     query: input.query,
246:   });
247: 
248:   return createEmptyQueryPlan({
249:     intent,
250:     complexity: input.complexity,
251:     artifactClass: input.artifactClass,
252:     authorityNeed,
253:     selectedChannels: selected,
254:     skippedChannels,
255:     routingReasons: uniqueStrings([
256:       `artifact:${input.artifactClass}`,
257:       `channels:${selected.join('+') || 'none'}`,
258:       ...(input.routingReasons ?? []),
259:     ]),
260:     fallbackPolicy: input.fallbackPolicy ?? DEFAULT_FALLBACK_POLICY,
261:   });
262: }
263: 
264: function uniqueStrings(values: string[]): string[] {
265:   return [...new Set(values.filter((value) => value.length > 0))];
266: }
267: 
268: function mergeSkippedChannels(
269:   left: QueryPlanSkippedChannel[],
270:   right: QueryPlanSkippedChannel[],
271: ): QueryPlanSkippedChannel[] {
272:   const seen = new Set<string>();
273:   const merged: QueryPlanSkippedChannel[] = [];
274:   for (const item of [...left, ...right]) {
275:     const key = `${item.channel}:${item.reason}`;
276:     if (seen.has(key)) continue;
277:     seen.add(key);
278:     merged.push(item);
279:   }
280:   return merged;
281: }
282: 
283: function roundMetric(value: number): number {
284:   return Math.round(value * 1000) / 1000;
285: }
286: 
287: export {
288:   type QueryPlan,
289:   type QueryPlanAuthorityNeed,
290:   type QueryPlanComplexity,
291:   type QueryPlanFallbackMode,
292:   type QueryPlanFallbackPolicy,
293:   type QueryPlanIntent,
294:   type QueryPlanSkippedChannel,
295:   createEmptyQueryPlan,
296:   buildComplexityQueryPlan,
297:   buildIntentQueryPlan,
298:   buildRoutingQueryPlan,
299:   inferAuthorityNeed,
300:   mergeQueryPlans,
301: };

(End of file - total 301 lines)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Content Normalizer
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Content-aware memory filename generation
5: // Smarter memory content generation
6: //
7: // Purpose: Normalize raw markdown content before it is passed to
8: // Embedding generation or BM25 indexing.  Raw markdown contains
9: // Structural noise (YAML frontmatter, HTML comment anchors, pipe
10: // Table syntax, fence markers, checkbox notation) that degrades
11: // The quality of semantic embeddings and keyword retrieval.
12: //
13: // Integration points (do NOT modify those files here — reference only):
14: //   - memory-parser.ts  ~line 159  : `content` is assigned from readFileWithEncoding()
15: // → wrap with normalizeContentForEmbedding() before passing to generateDocumentEmbedding()
16: //   - memory-save.ts    ~line 1093 : before generateDocumentEmbedding(parsed.content)
17: // → normalizeContentForEmbedding(parsed.content)
18: //   - bm25-index.ts     ~line 245  : where `content_text` is used for token building
19: // → normalizeContentForBM25(content_text)
20: //
21: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: // 1. PRIMITIVE STRIP / NORMALIZE HELPERS
23: 
24: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25: /**
26:  * Strip YAML frontmatter block from the top of a markdown file.
27:  *
28:  * Frontmatter is already parsed separately by memory-parser.ts into
29:  * structured fields (title, trigger_phrases, context_type, etc.), so
30:  * including it verbatim in the embedding text just adds repetitive
31:  * key-value noise.
32:  *
33:  * Matches the canonical `---\n…\n---` form that starts at the very
34:  * first character of the document.
35:  */
36: export function stripYamlFrontmatter(content: string): string {
37:   // Must start at position 0.  The closing `---` must be on its own line.
38:   // Without the `m` flag, `^` anchors to position 0 only (document start),
39:   // Preventing accidental matches on mid-document `---` (e.g. HR rules).
40:   return content.replace(/^---[\s\S]*?\n---\s*\n?/, '');
41: }
42: 
43: /**
44:  * Strip ANCHOR comment markers while preserving the text inside them.
45:  *
46:  * Examples removed:
47:  *   <!-- ANCHOR: state -->
48:  *   <!-- /ANCHOR: state -->
49:  *   <!-- ANCHOR:next-steps -->
50:  */
51: export function stripAnchors(content: string): string {
52:   // Opening and closing anchor tags — keep nothing (structural only)
53:   return content.replace(/<!--\s*\/?ANCHOR:[^>]*-->/gi, '');
54: }
55: 
56: /**
57:  * Strip all HTML comments.
58:  *
59:  * This catches structural markers not covered by stripAnchors(), e.g.:
60:  * <!-- gate: open -->
61:  *   <!-- TODO: remove -->
62:  *   <!-- prettier-ignore -->
63:  */
64: export function stripHtmlComments(content: string): string {
65:   // Non-greedy match to avoid swallowing multiple comments in one pass
66:   return content.replace(/<!--[\s\S]*?-->/g, '');
67: }
68: 
69: /**
70:  * Strip code-fence markers while retaining the code body.
71:  *
72:  * Before:
73:  *   ```typescript
74:  *   const x = 1;
75:  *   ```
76:  *
77:  * After:
78:  *   const x = 1;
79:  *
80:  * The language identifier on the opening fence is discarded because
81:  * tokens like "typescript" or "json" carry no semantic signal about
82:  * the stored knowledge.
83:  */
84: export function stripCodeFences(content: string): string {
85:   // Match ``` optionally followed by a language identifier, then any content,
86:   // Then a closing ```.  The 's' flag makes '.' match newlines.
87:   return content.replace(/^```[^\n]*\n([\s\S]*?)^```\s*$/gm, '$1');
88: }
89: 
90: /**
91:  * Convert markdown pipe tables to plain prose.
92:  *
93:  * Separator rows (e.g. `| --- | --- |`) are dropped entirely.
94:  * Data rows have their cell content extracted and joined with spaces.
95:  *
96:  * Before:
97:  *   | Tool | Purpose | Status |
98:  *   | ---- | ------- | ------ |
99:  *   | Grep | search  | active |
100:  *
101:  * After:
102:  *   Tool Purpose Status
103:  *   Grep search active
104:  */
105: export function normalizeMarkdownTables(content: string): string {
106:   const lines = content.split('\n');
107:   const result: string[] = [];
108: 
109:   for (const line of lines) {
110:     const trimmed = line.trim();
111: 
112:     // Separator row — pure dashes, colons, pipes, spaces
113:     if (/^\|[\s|:-]+\|$/.test(trimmed)) {
114:       continue;
115:     }
116: 
117:     // Data / header row starting and ending with pipe
118:     if (trimmed.startsWith('|') && trimmed.endsWith('|')) {
119:       const cells = trimmed
120:         .slice(1, -1)               // remove leading and trailing pipe
121:         .split('|')
122:         .map(cell => cell.trim())
123:         .filter(cell => cell.length > 0 && !/^[-:\s]+$/.test(cell)); // skip pure-separator cells
124: 
125:       if (cells.length > 0) {
126:         result.push(cells.join(' '));
127:       }
128:       continue;
129:     }
130: 
131:     result.push(line);
132:   }
133: 
134:   return result.join('\n');
135: }
136: 
137: /**
138:  * Normalize markdown list notation to clean prose tokens.
139:  *
140:  * Handles:
141:  * GitHub-style task checkboxes: `- [ ] [P] Implement…` → `[ ] [P] Implement…`
142:  *   - Checked checkboxes:            `- [x] Done item`             →  `[x] Done item`
143:  *   - Plain list bullets:            `- item` / `* item`           →  `item`
144:  *   - Ordered list numbers:          `1. item`                      →  `item`
145:  *
146:  * Leading whitespace (indentation for nested lists) is preserved so
147:  * that hierarchical structure is still represented via spacing.
148:  */
149: export function normalizeMarkdownLists(content: string): string {
150:   return content
151:     // Preserve checklist state markers while dropping the bullet itself.
152:     .replace(/^(\s*)[-*]\s+(\[[ xX]\])\s*/gm, '$1$2 ')
153:     // Plain unordered list bullets
154:     .replace(/^(\s*)[-*]\s+/gm, '$1')
155:     // Ordered list numbers
156:     .replace(/^(\s*)\d+\.\s+/gm, '$1');
157: }
158: 
159: /**
160:  * Convert markdown headings to plain section labels without hash marks.
161:  *
162:  * Before:  `## 3. SCOPE`
163:  * After:   `SCOPE`
164:  *
165:  * The numeric prefix (e.g. "3.") is also stripped because section
166:  * numbering carries no semantic meaning for retrieval.
167:  */
168: export function normalizeHeadings(content: string): string {
169:   return content.replace(
170:     /^#{1,6}\s+(?:\d+\.\s+)?(.+)$/gm,
171:     (_match, heading: string) => heading.trim()
172:   );
173: }
174: 
175: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
176: // 2. WHITESPACE CLEANUP
177: 
178: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
179: /**
180:  * Collapse runs of blank lines to a single blank line, and trim
181:  * leading / trailing whitespace from the whole document.
182:  *
183:  * This is applied as the final step in all public normalization
184:  * functions to ensure a clean, compact result.
185:  */
186: function collapseWhitespace(content: string): string {
187:   return content
188:     .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')   // 3+ consecutive newlines → 2
189:     .trim();
190: }
191: 
192: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
193: // 3. PUBLIC COMPOSITE FUNCTIONS
194: 
195: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196: /**
197:  * Normalize markdown content for use in embedding generation.
198:  *
199:  * Applies the full normalization pipeline in order:
200:  *   1. Strip YAML frontmatter      — already parsed into structured fields
201:  *   2. Strip anchor markers        — structural noise
202:  *   3. Strip remaining HTML comments — structural noise
203:  *   4. Strip code-fence markers    — keep code body, drop ``` + lang id
204:  *   5. Normalize pipe tables       — extract cell text
205:  *   6. Normalize list notation     — drop bullet / checkbox syntax
206:  *   7. Normalize headings          — drop `##` hash marks
207:  *   8. Collapse excess whitespace  — final cleanup
208:  *
209:  * The result is a clean prose representation suitable for semantic
210:  * embedding models (current local defaults and compatible providers).
211:  *
212:  * Integration point:
213:  *   memory-parser.ts  ~line 159 after `readFileWithEncoding()`,
214:  *   memory-save.ts    ~line 1093 before `generateDocumentEmbedding()`.
215:  */
216: export function normalizeContentForEmbedding(content: string): string {
217:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
218:     return '';
219:   }
220: 
221:   let normalized = content;
222:   normalized = stripYamlFrontmatter(normalized);
223:   normalized = stripAnchors(normalized);
224:   normalized = stripHtmlComments(normalized);
225:   normalized = stripCodeFences(normalized);
226:   normalized = normalizeMarkdownTables(normalized);
227:   normalized = normalizeMarkdownLists(normalized);
228:   normalized = normalizeHeadings(normalized);
229:   normalized = collapseWhitespace(normalized);
230: 
231:   return normalized;
232: }
233: 
234: /**
235:  * Normalize markdown content for use in BM25 keyword indexing.
236:  *
237:  * BM25 benefits from the same structural noise removal as the
238:  * embedding pipeline.  Code fence markers (``` + lang id) are stripped
239:  * but the code body is preserved so that exact identifier tokens
240:  * (function names, class names, CLI flags) remain searchable.
241:  *
242:  * Pipeline:
243:  *   1. Strip YAML frontmatter
244:  *   2. Strip anchor markers
245:  *   3. Strip remaining HTML comments
246:  *   4. Strip code-fence markers only (keep body)
247:  *   5. Normalize pipe tables
248:  *   6. Normalize list notation
249:  *   7. Normalize headings
250:  *   8. Collapse excess whitespace
251:  *
252:  * Integration point:
253:  *   bm25-index.ts ~line 245 where `content_text` is used for token building.
254:  */
255: export function normalizeContentForBM25(content: string): string {
256:   // The BM25 pipeline currently delegates to the same normalization
257:   // Steps as the embedding pipeline (frontmatter, anchors, HTML
258:   // Comments, code fences, tables, lists, headings, whitespace).
259:   // A separate entry point exists so BM25-specific adjustments
260:   // (e.g. preserving backtick inline code, adding stemming hints)
261:   // Can diverge in the future without modifying the embedding path.
262:   return normalizeContentForEmbedding(content);
263: }

(End of file - total 263 lines)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Anchor Chunker
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Anchor-aware chunk thinning
5: // Splits large memory files into chunks using ANCHOR tags as
6: // Natural boundaries. Falls back to structure-aware markdown
7: // Splitting when anchors are absent.
8: import { chunkMarkdown } from '@spec-kit/shared/lib/structure-aware-chunker';
9: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10:    1. TYPES
11: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
12: 
13: /**
14:  * Describes the AnchorChunk shape.
15:  */
16: export interface AnchorChunk {
17:   /** The text content of this chunk */
18:   content: string;
19:   /** Anchor IDs covered by this chunk (empty for fallback chunks) */
20:   anchorIds: string[];
21:   /** Human-readable label for this chunk */
22:   label: string;
23:   /** Approximate character count */
24:   charCount: number;
25: }
26: 
27: /**
28:  * Describes the ChunkingResult shape.
29:  */
30: export interface ChunkingResult {
31:   /** Strategy used: 'anchor' or 'structure' */
32:   strategy: 'anchor' | 'structure';
33:   /** The chunks produced */
34:   chunks: AnchorChunk[];
35:   /** Summary text for the parent record (first ~500 chars) */
36:   parentSummary: string;
37: }
38: 
39: interface AnchorSection {
40:   id: string | null;
41:   content: string;
42:   charCount: number;
43: }
44: 
45: interface ExtractedAnchorSections {
46:   sections: AnchorSection[];
47:   anchorCount: number;
48: }
49: 
50: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
51:    2. CONSTANTS
52: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
53: 
54: /** Target chunk size in characters (~1000 tokens at 4 chars/token) */
55: const TARGET_CHUNK_CHARS = 4000;
56: 
57: /** Maximum chunk size — hard cap to stay under embedding token budget */
58: const MAX_CHUNK_CHARS = 12000;
59: 
60: /** Minimum file size (chars) to trigger chunking */
61: export const CHUNKING_THRESHOLD = 50000;
62: 
63: /** Max characters for parent summary (BM25 fallback) */
64: const PARENT_SUMMARY_LENGTH = 500;
65: 
66: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67:    3. ANCHOR EXTRACTION
68: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
69: 
70: const ANCHOR_OPEN_RE = /<!--\s*(?:ANCHOR|anchor):\s*([^>\s]+)\s*-->/g;
71: 
72: /**
73:  * Extract anchor sections from content.
74:  * Returns sections between <!-- ANCHOR:id --> and <!-- /ANCHOR:id --> pairs,
75:  * while preserving uncovered text between anchors as unanchored sections.
76:  */
77: function extractAnchorSections(content: string): ExtractedAnchorSections {
78:   const sections: AnchorSection[] = [];
79:   const openPattern = new RegExp(ANCHOR_OPEN_RE.source, 'gi');
80:   let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
81:   let cursor = 0;
82:   let anchorCount = 0;
83: 
84:   while ((match = openPattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
85:     const anchorId = match[1].trim();
86:     const startPos = match.index;
87: 
88:     // Find closing tag
89:     const escapeRegex = (str: string): string => str.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
90:     const closingPattern = new RegExp(
91:       `<!--\\s*/(?:ANCHOR|anchor):\\s*${escapeRegex(anchorId)}\\s*-->`,
92:       'i'
93:     );
94:     const afterOpen = content.slice(startPos + match[0].length);
95:     const closeMatch = closingPattern.exec(afterOpen);
96: 
97:     if (closeMatch) {
98:       if (startPos > cursor) {
99:         const uncovered = content.slice(cursor, startPos);
100:         if (uncovered.trim()) {
101:           sections.push({
102:             id: null,
103:             content: uncovered,
104:             charCount: uncovered.length,
105:           });
106:         }
107:       }
108: 
109:       const endPos = startPos + match[0].length + closeMatch.index + closeMatch[0].length;
110:       const sectionContent = content.slice(startPos, endPos);
111: 
112:       sections.push({
113:         id: anchorId,
114:         content: sectionContent,
115:         charCount: sectionContent.length,
116:       });
117:       anchorCount++;
118:       cursor = endPos;
119:       openPattern.lastIndex = endPos;
120:     }
121:   }
122: 
123:   if (cursor < content.length) {
124:     const trailing = content.slice(cursor);
125:     if (trailing.trim()) {
126:       sections.push({
127:         id: null,
128:         content: trailing,
129:         charCount: trailing.length,
130:       });
131:     }
132:   }
133: 
134:   return { sections, anchorCount };
135: }
136: 
137: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
138:    4. ANCHOR-BASED CHUNKING
139: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
140: 
141: /**
142:  * Group anchor sections into chunks that stay under the target size.
143:  * Small adjacent anchors are merged; large anchors become their own chunk.
144:  */
145: function chunkByAnchors(sections: AnchorSection[]): AnchorChunk[] {
146:   const chunks: AnchorChunk[] = [];
147:   let currentContent = '';
148:   let currentAnchors: string[] = [];
149:   let currentLabels: string[] = [];
150:   let currentSize = 0;
151:   let unanchoredIndex = 0;
152: 
153:   function getSectionLabel(section: AnchorSection): string {
154:     if (section.id) {
155:       return section.id;
156:     }
157:     unanchoredIndex++;
158:     return `unanchored-${unanchoredIndex}`;
159:   }
160: 
161:   function flush(): void {
162:     if (currentContent.trim()) {
163:       const label = currentAnchors.length === 1
164:         ? currentAnchors[0]
165:         : currentAnchors.length > 1
166:           ? `${currentAnchors[0]}..${currentAnchors[currentAnchors.length - 1]}`
167:           : currentLabels.length === 1
168:             ? currentLabels[0]
169:             : `${currentLabels[0]}..${currentLabels[currentLabels.length - 1]}`;
170: 
171:       chunks.push({
172:         content: currentContent.trim(),
173:         anchorIds: [...currentAnchors],
174:         label,
175:         charCount: currentContent.length,
176:       });
177:     }
178:     currentContent = '';
179:     currentAnchors = [];
180:     currentLabels = [];
181:     currentSize = 0;
182:   }
183: 
184:   for (const section of sections) {
185:     const sectionLabel = getSectionLabel(section);
186: 
187:     // Oversized anchor sections must be further split to respect
188:     // MAX_CHUNK_CHARS. Previously they were emitted as single oversized chunks.
189:     if (section.charCount > MAX_CHUNK_CHARS) {
190:       flush();
191:       // Split oversized content into MAX_CHUNK_CHARS-sized sub-chunks
192:       const lines = section.content.split('\n');
193:       let subContent = '';
194:       let subIdx = 0;
195:       for (const line of lines) {
196:         if (subContent.length + line.length + 1 > MAX_CHUNK_CHARS && subContent.trim()) {
197:           chunks.push({
198:             content: subContent.trim(),
199:             anchorIds: section.id ? [section.id] : [],
200:             label: section.id ? `${section.id}:${subIdx}` : `${sectionLabel}:${subIdx}`,
201:             charCount: subContent.length,
202:           });
203:           subContent = '';
204:           subIdx++;
205:         }
206:         // Handle single lines exceeding MAX_CHUNK_CHARS: split at char boundary
207:         if (line.length > MAX_CHUNK_CHARS && !subContent) {
208:           for (let c = 0; c < line.length; c += MAX_CHUNK_CHARS) {
209:             const slice = line.slice(c, c + MAX_CHUNK_CHARS);
210:             chunks.push({
211:               content: slice,
212:               anchorIds: section.id ? [section.id] : [],
213:               label: section.id ? `${section.id}:${subIdx}` : `${sectionLabel}:${subIdx}`,
214:               charCount: slice.length,
215:             });
216:             subIdx++;
217:           }
218:           continue;
219:         }
220:         subContent += (subContent ? '\n' : '') + line;
221:       }
222:       if (subContent.trim()) {
223:         chunks.push({
224:           content: subContent.trim(),
225:           anchorIds: section.id ? [section.id] : [],
226:           label: subIdx > 0
227:             ? (section.id ? `${section.id}:${subIdx}` : `${sectionLabel}:${subIdx}`)
228:             : sectionLabel,
229:           charCount: subContent.length,
230:         });
231:       }
232:       continue;
233:     }
234: 
235:     // Would adding this section exceed the target?
236:     if (currentSize + section.charCount > TARGET_CHUNK_CHARS && currentContent.trim()) {
237:       flush();
238:     }
239: 
240:     currentContent += (currentContent ? '\n\n' : '') + section.content;
241:     currentLabels.push(sectionLabel);
242:     if (section.id) {
243:       currentAnchors.push(section.id);
244:     }
245:     currentSize += section.charCount;
246:   }
247: 
248:   flush();
249:   return chunks;
250: }
251: 
252: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
253:    5. STRUCTURE-BASED FALLBACK CHUNKING
254: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
255: 
256: /**
257:  * Fall back to splitting by markdown headings and paragraphs.
258:  * Uses the shared AST-aware markdown chunker so fenced code and tables remain atomic.
259:  */
260: function chunkByStructure(content: string): AnchorChunk[] {
261:   const maxTokens = Math.floor(TARGET_CHUNK_CHARS / 4);
262: 
263:   return chunkMarkdown(content, maxTokens).map((chunk, index) => ({
264:     content: chunk.content,
265:     anchorIds: [],
266:     label: `chunk-${index + 1}`,
267:     charCount: chunk.content.length,
268:   }));
269: }
270: 
271: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
272:    6. MAIN ENTRY POINT
273: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
274: 
275: /**
276:  * Chunk a large memory file into smaller pieces for indexing.
277:  *
278:  * Strategy:
279:  * 1. Try anchor-based splitting first (files with ANCHOR tags)
280:  * 2. Fall back to structure-aware markdown splitting
281:  *
282:  * @param content - The full file content
283:  * @returns ChunkingResult with strategy info and chunk array
284:  */
285: export function chunkLargeFile(content: string): ChunkingResult {
286:   const parentSummary = content.slice(0, PARENT_SUMMARY_LENGTH).trim();
287: 
288:   // Try anchor-based chunking first
289:   const { sections: anchorSections, anchorCount } = extractAnchorSections(content);
290: 
291:   if (anchorCount >= 2) {
292:     const chunks = chunkByAnchors(anchorSections);
293:     if (chunks.length >= 2) {
294:       return { strategy: 'anchor', chunks, parentSummary };
295:     }
296:   }
297: 
298:   // Fall back to structure-based chunking
299:   const chunks = chunkByStructure(content);
300:   return { strategy: 'structure', chunks, parentSummary };
301: }
302: 
303: /**
304:  * Check if a file's content exceeds the chunking threshold.
305:  */
306: export function needsChunking(content: string): boolean {
307:   return content.length > CHUNKING_THRESHOLD;
308: }

(End of file - total 308 lines)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Learned Triggers Schema Migration
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Learned relevance feedback
5: // Schema migration for the learned_triggers column.
6: //
7: // ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN learned_triggers TEXT DEFAULT '[]';
8: //
9: // CRITICAL: This column MUST NOT be added to the FTS5 index.
10: // The column stores JSON array of { term, addedAt, source, expiresAt }
11: // Objects representing learned relevance feedback terms.
12: //
13: // Rollback: ALTER TABLE memory_index DROP COLUMN learned_triggers;
14: // (SQLite 3.35.0+)
15: import type { DatabaseExtended as Database } from '@spec-kit/shared/types';
16: 
17: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18: // 1. TYPES
19: 
20: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: /**
22:  * A single learned trigger entry stored in the learned_triggers JSON array.
23:  */
24: export interface LearnedTriggerEntry {
25:   /** The learned term */
26:   term: string;
27:   /** Unix timestamp (seconds) when this term was added */
28:   addedAt: number;
29:   /** Source identifier (e.g., query ID that led to this learning) */
30:   source: string;
31:   /** Unix timestamp (seconds) when this term expires (30-day TTL) */
32:   expiresAt: number;
33: }
34: 
35: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: // 2. CONSTANTS
37: 
38: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39: /** Column name for learned triggers (NOT in FTS5 index) */
40: export const LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN = 'learned_triggers';
41: 
42: /** Default value for the learned_triggers column */
43: export const LEARNED_TRIGGERS_DEFAULT = '[]';
44: 
45: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46: // 3. MIGRATION
47: 
48: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49: /**
50:  * Add the learned_triggers column to memory_index if it does not already exist.
51:  * This migration is idempotent -- safe to run multiple times.
52:  *
53:  * CRITICAL: This column is stored on memory_index only. It is NOT added to
54:  * the memory_fts FTS5 virtual table. The FTS5 index columns are:
55:  *   title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text
56:  * learned_triggers is deliberately excluded to keep user-generated content
57:  * isolated from the organic search index.
58:  *
59:  * @param db - SQLite database connection
60:  * @returns true if migration ran (column was added), false if already present
61:  */
62: export function migrateLearnedTriggers(db: Database): boolean {
63:   try {
64:     // Check if column already exists using PRAGMA table_info
65:     const columns = db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
66:     const hasColumn = columns.some((col) => col.name === LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN);
67: 
68:     if (hasColumn) {
69:       return false; // Already migrated
70:     }
71: 
72:     db.exec(
73:       `ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN ${LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN} TEXT DEFAULT '${LEARNED_TRIGGERS_DEFAULT}'`
74:     );
75: 
76:     console.warn('[learned-triggers-schema] Migration complete: learned_triggers column added');
77:     return true;
78:   } catch (error: unknown) {
79:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
80:     // SQLite may report "duplicate column name" if run concurrently
81:     if (msg.includes('duplicate column')) {
82:       return false;
83:     }
84:     console.error(`[learned-triggers-schema] Migration failed: ${msg}`);
85:     throw error;
86:   }
87: }
88: 
89: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90: // 4. FTS5 ISOLATION VERIFICATION
91: 
92: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
93: /**
94:  * CRITICAL test: Verify that learned_triggers is NOT present in the
95:  * FTS5 index (memory_fts). This is a safety check that should be run
96:  * after migration and during tests.
97:  *
98:  * The FTS5 virtual table memory_fts should only contain:
99:  *   title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text
100:  *
101:  * If learned_triggers appears in FTS5, it means user-generated feedback
102:  * has polluted the organic search index -- a data integrity violation.
103:  *
104:  * @param db - SQLite database connection
105:  * @returns true if isolation is verified (learned_triggers NOT in FTS5)
106:  * @throws Error if learned_triggers IS found in FTS5 (critical violation)
107:  */
108: export function verifyFts5Isolation(db: Database): boolean {
109:   try {
110:     // Check if FTS5 table exists
111:     const ftsTable = db.prepare(
112:       `SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'`
113:     ).get() as { sql: string } | undefined;
114: 
115:     if (!ftsTable) {
116:       // FTS5 table does not exist; isolation trivially holds
117:       return true;
118:     }
119: 
120:     const createSql = ftsTable.sql.toLowerCase();
121: 
122:     // Check that learned_triggers does NOT appear in the FTS5 CREATE statement
123:     if (createSql.includes('learned_triggers')) {
124:       throw new Error(
125:         'CRITICAL: learned_triggers found in FTS5 index (memory_fts). ' +
126:         'This violates data isolation requirements. Learned triggers must ' +
127:         'ONLY exist in the memory_index.learned_triggers column, never in FTS5.'
128:       );
129:     }
130: 
131:     return true;
132:   } catch (error: unknown) {
133:     if (error instanceof Error && error.message.startsWith('CRITICAL:')) {
134:       throw error; // Re-throw critical violations
135:     }
136:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
137:     console.error(`[learned-triggers-schema] FTS5 isolation check failed: ${msg}`);
138:     return false;
139:   }
140: }
141: 
142: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143: // 5. ROLLBACK
144: 
145: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146: /**
147:  * Drop the learned_triggers column from memory_index.
148:  * Requires SQLite 3.35.0+ which supports ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN.
149:  *
150:  * @param db - SQLite database connection
151:  * @returns true if column was dropped, false if it did not exist
152:  */
153: export function rollbackLearnedTriggers(db: Database): boolean {
154:   try {
155:     // Check if column exists first
156:     const columns = db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
157:     const hasColumn = columns.some((col) => col.name === LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN);
158: 
159:     if (!hasColumn) {
160:       return false; // Nothing to rollback
161:     }
162: 
163:     db.exec(`ALTER TABLE memory_index DROP COLUMN ${LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN}`);
164:     console.warn('[learned-triggers-schema] Rollback complete: learned_triggers column removed');
165:     return true;
166:   } catch (error: unknown) {
167:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
168:     console.error(`[learned-triggers-schema] Rollback failed: ${msg}`);
169:     throw error;
170:   }
171: }
172: 
173: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
174: // 6. HELPERS
175: 
176: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177: /**
178:  * Parse the learned_triggers JSON column value into typed entries.
179:  *
180:  * @param raw - Raw JSON string from the database column
181:  * @returns Parsed array of learned trigger entries
182:  */
183: export function parseLearnedTriggers(raw: string | null | undefined): LearnedTriggerEntry[] {
184:   if (!raw || raw === LEARNED_TRIGGERS_DEFAULT) {
185:     return [];
186:   }
187:   try {
188:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
189:     if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return [];
190:     return parsed.filter(
191:       (e: unknown): e is LearnedTriggerEntry =>
192:         typeof e === 'object' && e !== null &&
193:         typeof (e as LearnedTriggerEntry).term === 'string' &&
194:         typeof (e as LearnedTriggerEntry).addedAt === 'number' &&
195:         typeof (e as LearnedTriggerEntry).expiresAt === 'number'
196:     );
197:   } catch {
198:     return [];
199:   }
200: }
201: 
202: /**
203:  * Serialize learned trigger entries to JSON for storage.
204:  *
205:  * @param entries - Array of learned trigger entries
206:  * @returns JSON string for database storage
207:  */
208: export function serializeLearnedTriggers(entries: LearnedTriggerEntry[]): string {
209:   return JSON.stringify(entries);
210: }

(End of file - total 210 lines)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Query Router
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Tier-to-channel-subset routing with BM25 and graph preservation overrides.
5: // Preserves authority-oriented retrieval signals without widening every query
6: // to the full search pipeline.
7: 
8: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
9: import {
10:   classifyQueryComplexity,
11:   isComplexityRouterEnabled,
12:   type QueryComplexityTier,
13:   type ClassificationResult,
14: } from './query-classifier.js';
15: import { getStrategyForQuery, type ArtifactClass } from './artifact-routing.js';
16: import * as intentClassifier from './intent-classifier.js';
17: import {
18:   buildRoutingQueryPlan,
19:   inferAuthorityNeed,
20:   mergeQueryPlans,
21:   type QueryPlan,
22: } from '../query/query-plan.js';
23: import * as vectorIndex from './vector-index.js';
24: import { getEntityDensityScore } from './entity-density.js';
25: import { recordInvocation } from './routing-telemetry.js';
26: 
27: // Feature catalog: Query complexity router
28: // Feature catalog: Query complexity router
29: 
30: 
31: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32:    1. TYPES & CONSTANTS
33: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
34: 
35: /** Channel names matching SOURCE_TYPES in rrf-fusion.ts */
36: type ChannelName = 'vector' | 'fts' | 'bm25' | 'graph' | 'degree';
37: 
38: /** Maps each complexity tier to the set of channels that should be executed. */
39: interface ChannelRoutingConfig {
40:   simple: ChannelName[];
41:   moderate: ChannelName[];
42:   complex: ChannelName[];
43: }
44: 
45: /** Result of the routeQuery convenience function. */
46: interface RouteResult {
47:   tier: QueryComplexityTier;
48:   channels: ChannelName[];
49:   classification: ClassificationResult;
50:   queryPlan: QueryPlan;
51:   qualityFallback: QualityGapFallbackPlan;
52: }
53: 
54: interface QualityGapSignal {
55:   quality?: string;
56:   avgScore?: number;
57:   avg_score?: number;
58: }
59: 
60: interface QualityGapFallbackPlan {
61:   engaged: boolean;
62:   reason: string | null;
63:   deadlineMs: number;
64:   tiers: Array<'fts5' | 'bm25' | 'grep'>;
65: }
66: 
67: /** All available channels in execution order. */
68: const ALL_CHANNELS: readonly ChannelName[] = ['vector', 'fts', 'bm25', 'graph', 'degree'] as const;
69: 
70: /** Minimum required channels for safe routing. */
71: const MIN_CHANNELS = 2;
72: 
73: /** Fallback channels used to pad configs that violate the minimum invariant. */
74: const FALLBACK_CHANNELS: readonly ChannelName[] = ['vector', 'fts'] as const;
75: const BM25_PRESERVING_ARTIFACTS = new Set([
76:   'spec',
77:   'plan',
78:   'tasks',
79:   'checklist',
80:   'decision-record',
81:   'implementation-summary',
82:   'research',
83: ]);
84: 
85: /** Entity-density threshold: ≥2 query terms hitting high-degree memory rows. */
86: const ENTITY_DENSITY_ACTIVATION_THRESHOLD = 2;
87: const QUALITY_GAP_AVG_SCORE_THRESHOLD = 0.20;
88: const QUALITY_GAP_FALLBACK_DEADLINE_MS = 200;
89: const MAX_ROUTING_REASON_LENGTH = 120;
90: 
91: let _hasWarnedInvalidGraphPreservationFlag = false;
92: const _safeGetDbWarnedClasses = new Set<string>();
93: 
94: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95:    2. DEFAULT ROUTING CONFIG
96: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
97: 
98: /**
99:  * Default tier-to-channel mapping:
100:  * - simple:   2 channels (vector + fts) — fastest path
101:  * - moderate: 3 channels (vector + fts + bm25) — balanced
102:  * - complex:  5 channels (all) — full pipeline
103:  */
104: const DEFAULT_ROUTING_CONFIG: ChannelRoutingConfig = {
105:   simple: ['vector', 'fts'],
106:   moderate: ['vector', 'fts', 'bm25'],
107:   complex: ['vector', 'fts', 'bm25', 'graph', 'degree'],
108: };
109: 
110: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
111:    3. CHANNEL SUBSET RESOLUTION
112: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
113: 
114: /**
115:  * Enforce the minimum 2-distinct-channel invariant on a channel list.
116:  * If the list has fewer than MIN_CHANNELS entries, pad with
117:  * fallback channels (vector, fts) until the minimum is met.
118:  */
119: function enforceMinimumChannels(channels: ChannelName[]): ChannelName[] {
120:   const result = [...new Set(channels)] as ChannelName[];
121:   if (result.length >= MIN_CHANNELS) return result;
122: 
123:   for (const fallback of FALLBACK_CHANNELS) {
124:     if (result.length >= MIN_CHANNELS) break;
125:     if (!result.includes(fallback)) {
126:       result.push(fallback);
127:     }
128:   }
129: 
130:   return result;
131: }
132: 
133: /**
134:  * Get the channel subset for a given complexity tier.
135:  *
136:  * @param tier - The classified query complexity tier
137:  * @param config - Optional custom routing config (defaults to DEFAULT_ROUTING_CONFIG)
138:  * @returns Array of channel names to execute, guaranteed minimum 2 channels
139:  */
140: function getChannelSubset(
141:   tier: QueryComplexityTier,
142:   config?: ChannelRoutingConfig,
143: ): ChannelName[] {
144:   const effectiveConfig = config ?? DEFAULT_ROUTING_CONFIG;
145:   const channels = effectiveConfig[tier] ?? [...ALL_CHANNELS];
146:   return enforceMinimumChannels([...channels]);
147: }
148: 
149: /**
150:  * Decides whether BM25 should be preserved independently of complexity tier.
151:  *
152:  * Intent triggers (`find_spec`, `find_decision`) preserve BM25 even for simple
153:  * queries because exact lexical matches matter for spec and ADR retrieval.
154:  * Artifact-class authority preserves BM25 for documentation packets with
155:  * durable source-of-truth semantics. `precomputedIntent` lets callers reuse an
156:  * already-classified intent and skip duplicate classifier work.
157:  *
158:  * @param query - Raw search query.
159:  * @param precomputedIntent - Optional intent label already computed by caller.
160:  * @returns True when BM25 should be added to the selected channel set.
161:  */
162: function shouldPreserveBm25(query: string, precomputedIntent?: string): boolean {
163:   const intent = precomputedIntent ?? intentClassifier.classifyIntent(query).intent;
164:   if (intent === 'find_spec' || intent === 'find_decision') {
165:     return true;
166:   }
167: 
168:   const artifact = resolveArtifactClass(query);
169:   return BM25_PRESERVING_ARTIFACTS.has(artifact);
170: }
171: 
172: /**
173:  * Reads the default-ON graph preservation feature flag.
174:  *
175:  * Unset values enable preservation. `"1"`, `"true"`, `"yes"`, and `"on"`
176:  * enable it; `"0"`, `"false"`, `"no"`, `"off"`, and `""` disable it. Unknown
177:  * values default to enabled and warn once so operators can spot typos without
178:  * losing the safe default.
179:  *
180:  * @returns True when graph-channel preservation should be active.
181:  */
182: function isGraphChannelPreservationEnabled(): boolean {
183:   const raw = process.env.SPECKIT_GRAPH_CHANNEL_PRESERVATION;
184:   if (raw === undefined) return true;
185: 
186:   const normalized = raw.trim().toLowerCase();
187:   if (['0', 'false', 'no', 'off', ''].includes(normalized)) return false;
188:   if (['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes(normalized)) return true;
189: 
190:   if (!_hasWarnedInvalidGraphPreservationFlag) {
191:     console.warn(
192:       '[query-router] Unrecognized SPECKIT_GRAPH_CHANNEL_PRESERVATION value; defaulting to enabled:',
193:       raw,
194:     );
195:     _hasWarnedInvalidGraphPreservationFlag = true;
196:   }
197:   return true;
198: }
199: 
200: interface GraphPreservationDecision {
201:   preserved: boolean;
202:   reasons: string[];
203:   includeDegree: boolean;
204: }
205: 
206: /**
207:  * Decide whether the graph channel should be added to a
208:  * routing decision regardless of complexity tier.
209:  *
210:  * The feature flag self-gates this helper so direct callers see the same
211:  * disabled behavior as routeQuery. Intent gates preserve graph for `find_spec`
212:  * and `find_decision`; entity-density gates preserve graph when at least two
213:  * query terms hit high-degree memory rows. `precomputedIntent` lets routeQuery
214:  * pass the already-classified intent and avoid duplicate classifier work.
215:  *
216:  * - Intent-driven: `find_decision` or `find_spec` → preserved.
217:  * - Entity-density: ≥2 query terms exact-match titles/triggers of memory_index
218:  *   rows with ≥3 outgoing causal_edges → preserved + degree.
219:  *
220:  * Cold-start (empty causal_edges or missing DB): the entity-density signal
221:  * scores 0 and the override stays inactive.
222:  *
223:  * @param query - Raw search query.
224:  * @param db - Optional database handle used for entity-density scoring.
225:  * @param precomputedIntent - Optional intent label already computed by caller.
226:  * @returns Graph preservation decision and reason labels.
227:  */
228: function shouldPreserveGraph(
229:   query: string,
230:   db: Database.Database | null,
231:   precomputedIntent?: string,
232: ): GraphPreservationDecision {
233:   if (!isGraphChannelPreservationEnabled()) {
234:     return { preserved: false, reasons: [], includeDegree: false };
235:   }
236: 
237:   const reasons: string[] = [];
238:   let preserved = false;
239:   let includeDegree = false;
240: 
241:   const intent = precomputedIntent ?? intentClassifier.classifyIntent(query).intent;
242:   if (intent === 'find_spec' || intent === 'find_decision') {
243:     preserved = true;
244:     reasons.push('graph-preserved-by-intent');
245:   }
246: 
247:   const densityScore = getEntityDensityScore(query, db);
248:   if (densityScore >= ENTITY_DENSITY_ACTIVATION_THRESHOLD) {
249:     preserved = true;
250:     includeDegree = true;
251:     reasons.push('graph-preserved-by-entity-density');
252:   }
253: 
254:   return { preserved, reasons, includeDegree };
255: }
256: 
257: /**
258:  * Resolve the vector-index database handle for optional entity-density routing.
259:  *
260:  * Database initialization can fail differently across boot states, so this
261:  * helper preserves the null-on-failure contract while warning once per error
262:  * class to keep repeated routing calls from flooding stderr.
263:  */
264: function safeGetDb(): Database.Database | null {
265:   try {
266:     return vectorIndex.getDb();
267:   } catch (err: unknown) {
268:     const errClass = err instanceof Error ? err.constructor.name : typeof err;
269:     if (!_safeGetDbWarnedClasses.has(errClass)) {
270:       _safeGetDbWarnedClasses.add(errClass);
271:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
272:       // eslint-disable-next-line no-console
273:       console.warn(`[query-router] safeGetDb failed (${errClass}): ${message}`);
274:     }
275:     return null;
276:   }
277: }
278: 
279: function resolveArtifactClass(query: string): ArtifactClass {
280:   try {
281:     return getStrategyForQuery(query)?.detectedClass ?? 'unknown';
282:   } catch {
283:     return 'unknown';
284:   }
285: }
286: 
287: function resolveAvgScore(signal: QualityGapSignal | undefined): number | null {
288:   const candidate = signal?.avgScore ?? signal?.avg_score;
289:   return typeof candidate === 'number' && Number.isFinite(candidate) ? candidate : null;
290: }
291: 
292: function buildQualityGapFallbackPlan(signal?: QualityGapSignal): QualityGapFallbackPlan {
293:   const avgScore = resolveAvgScore(signal);
294:   const quality = typeof signal?.quality === 'string' ? signal.quality.toLowerCase() : null;
295:   const engaged = quality === 'gap' && avgScore !== null && avgScore < QUALITY_GAP_AVG_SCORE_THRESHOLD;
296: 
297:   return {
298:     engaged,
299:     reason: engaged
300:       ? `QUALITY=gap and avg_score=${avgScore.toFixed(3)} below ${QUALITY_GAP_AVG_SCORE_THRESHOLD.toFixed(2)}`
301:       : null,
302:     deadlineMs: QUALITY_GAP_FALLBACK_DEADLINE_MS,
303:     tiers: engaged ? ['fts5', 'bm25', 'grep'] : [],
304:   };
305: }
306: 
307: function clampRoutingReason(reason: string): string {
308:   if (reason.length <= MAX_ROUTING_REASON_LENGTH) return reason;
309:   return `${reason.slice(0, MAX_ROUTING_REASON_LENGTH - 1)}…`;
310: }
311: 
312: function appendRoutingReason(reasons: string[], reason: string): void {
313:   const clamped = clampRoutingReason(reason);
314:   if (!reasons.includes(clamped)) reasons.push(clamped);
315: }
316: 
317: function clampRoutingReasons(reasons: readonly string[]): string[] {
318:   return reasons.map(clampRoutingReason);
319: }
320: 
321: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
322:    4. CONVENIENCE: CLASSIFY + ROUTE
323: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
324: 
325: /**
326:  * Classify a query's complexity and route it to the appropriate channel subset
327:  * in a single call.
328:  *
329:  * When the SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER feature flag is disabled, returns all 5 channels
330:  * (full pipeline) regardless of classification result.
331:  *
332:  * @param query - The search query to classify and route
333:  * @param triggerPhrases - Optional trigger phrases for simple-tier classification
334:  * @returns RouteResult with tier, channels, and full classification details
335:  */
336: function routeQuery(
337:   query: string,
338:   triggerPhrases?: string[],
339:   qualitySignal?: QualityGapSignal,
340: ): RouteResult {
341:   const classification = classifyQueryComplexity(query, triggerPhrases);
342:   const qualityFallback = buildQualityGapFallbackPlan(qualitySignal);
343:   const intent = intentClassifier.classifyIntent(query).intent;
344: 
345:   // When feature flag is disabled, classifier returns 'complex' with 'fallback' confidence.
346:   // In that case, always return all channels (full pipeline — safe default).
347:   if (!isComplexityRouterEnabled()) {
348:     const artifactClass = resolveArtifactClass(query);
349:     const routingPlan = buildRoutingQueryPlan({
350:       query,
351:       complexity: classification.tier,
352:       artifactClass,
353:       selectedChannels: ALL_CHANNELS,
354:       allChannels: ALL_CHANNELS,
355:       intent,
356:       authorityNeed: inferAuthorityNeed({ intent, artifactClass, query }),
357:       routingReasons: clampRoutingReasons([
358:         'complexity-router-disabled',
359:         ...(qualityFallback.engaged ? ['quality-gap-fallback-engaged'] : []),
360:       ]),
361:       fallbackPolicy: {
362:         mode: qualityFallback.engaged ? 'fts5_bm25_grep_broadening' : 'full_pipeline',
363:         reason: qualityFallback.reason ?? 'Complexity router disabled, preserving full pipeline behavior',
364:         ...(qualityFallback.engaged ? {
365:           tiers: qualityFallback.tiers,
366:           deadlineMs: qualityFallback.deadlineMs,
367:         } : {}),
368:       },
369:     });
370:     recordInvocation([...ALL_CHANNELS]);
371:     return {
372:       tier: classification.tier,
373:       channels: [...ALL_CHANNELS],
374:       classification,
375:       queryPlan: mergeQueryPlans(classification.queryPlan, routingPlan),
376:       qualityFallback,
377:     };
378:   }
379: 
380:   const channels = getChannelSubset(classification.tier);
381:   const artifactClass = resolveArtifactClass(query);
382: 
383:   // Existing override: BM25 preservation for simple-tier authority-focused
384:   // queries. Behavior unchanged.
385:   const preserveBm25 = classification.tier === 'simple' && shouldPreserveBm25(query, intent);
386:   let adjustedChannels = preserveBm25
387:     ? enforceMinimumChannels([...channels, 'bm25'])
388:     : channels;
389:   const routingReasons: string[] = [];
390:   if (
391:     preserveBm25
392:     && adjustedChannels.includes('bm25')
393:     && !channels.includes('bm25')
394:   ) {
395:     const reason = intent === 'find_spec' || intent === 'find_decision'
396:       ? 'bm25-preserved-by-intent'
397:       : 'bm25-preserved-by-artifact-class';
398:     appendRoutingReason(routingReasons, reason);
399:   }
400: 
401:   // Graph-channel preservation override. Adds the graph
402:   // channel to simple/moderate tiers when intent is find_spec/find_decision OR
403:   // when the entity-density signal fires. Complex-tier already includes graph
404:   // so the override is a no-op there. The default-on feature flag
405:   // keeps a clean revert path.
406:   if (isGraphChannelPreservationEnabled() && classification.tier !== 'complex') {
407:     const decision = shouldPreserveGraph(query, safeGetDb(), intent);
408:     if (decision.preserved) {
409:       const additions: ChannelName[] = ['graph'];
410:       if (decision.includeDegree) additions.push('degree');
411:       adjustedChannels = enforceMinimumChannels([...adjustedChannels, ...additions]);
412:       for (const reason of decision.reasons) {
413:         appendRoutingReason(routingReasons, reason);
414:       }
415:     }
416:   }
417: 
418:   const routingPlan = buildRoutingQueryPlan({
419:     query,
420:     complexity: classification.tier,
421:     artifactClass,
422:     selectedChannels: adjustedChannels,
423:     allChannels: ALL_CHANNELS,
424:     intent,
425:     authorityNeed: inferAuthorityNeed({ intent, artifactClass, query }),
426:     routingReasons: clampRoutingReasons([
427:       ...routingReasons,
428:       ...(qualityFallback.engaged ? ['quality-gap-fallback-engaged'] : []),
429:     ]),
430:     fallbackPolicy: qualityFallback.engaged ? {
431:       mode: 'fts5_bm25_grep_broadening',
432:       reason: qualityFallback.reason ?? 'QUALITY=gap fallback engaged',
433:       tiers: qualityFallback.tiers,
434:       deadlineMs: qualityFallback.deadlineMs,
435:     } : undefined,
436:   });
437: 
438:   recordInvocation(adjustedChannels);
439: 
440:   return {
441:     tier: classification.tier,
442:     channels: adjustedChannels,
443:     classification,
444:     queryPlan: mergeQueryPlans(classification.queryPlan, routingPlan),
445:     qualityFallback,
446:   };
447: }
448: 
449: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
450:    5. EXPORTS
451: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
452: 
453: export {
454:   // Types
455:   type ChannelName,
456:   type ChannelRoutingConfig,
457:   type RouteResult,
458: 
459:   // Constants
460:   DEFAULT_ROUTING_CONFIG,
461:   ALL_CHANNELS,
462:   MIN_CHANNELS,
463:   FALLBACK_CHANNELS,
464:   ENTITY_DENSITY_ACTIVATION_THRESHOLD,
465:   QUALITY_GAP_AVG_SCORE_THRESHOLD,
466:   QUALITY_GAP_FALLBACK_DEADLINE_MS,
467: 
468:   // Functions
469:   getChannelSubset,
470:   routeQuery,
471:   buildQualityGapFallbackPlan,
472:   shouldPreserveGraph,
473:   isGraphChannelPreservationEnabled,
474: 
475:   // Internal helpers (exported for testing)
476:   enforceMinimumChannels,
477: };
